title: PUT /books/{id} and DELETE /books/{id} return 200 OK for a non-existent id instead of 404
evidence: main.go:243-251 and main.go:260-267 branch on err.Error()=='sql: no rows in result set', but UpdateBook/DeleteBook use bs.db.Exec (main.go:106-116) which returns a nil error and never sql.ErrNoRows; RowsAffected is unchecked, so updates/deletes of missing ids silently succeed with 200
suggestion: Inspect res.RowsAffected() after Exec and return http.StatusNotFound when it is 0

title: POST /books returns "id":0 in the 201 response (created id not captured)
evidence: main.go:55-62 CreateBook discards the result of db.Exec so LastInsertId() is never read; the handler at main.go:183-192 echoes the decoded book whose ID is still 0
suggestion: Capture res.LastInsertId() into book.ID before encoding the 201 response (same for the PUT echo)

title: HTTP handlers, validation, health, and status codes are untested (coverage 32.5%)
evidence: main_test.go only calls BookStore methods directly; there is no net/http/httptest coverage; scores.json test_coverage=0.325. The two bugs above went undetected because no test hits the routes
suggestion: Add httptest.NewRecorder-based tests per route covering 201/200/400/404 and /health

title: DELETE returns a plain-text body instead of JSON
evidence: main.go:271-272 writes the string 'Book deleted successfully' with no JSON content type; the spec asks for JSON responses
suggestion: Return a JSON body such as {"status":"deleted"} with Content-Type application/json

title: Fragile not-found detection via string comparison
evidence: main.go:215, 245, 262 compare err.Error()=='sql: no rows in result set' rather than using errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows)
suggestion: Use errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) for robust sentinel-error checks
